commands.review_summary:
The PR preserves redacted Codex context-engine tool payloads, preloads daily memory for ordinary fresh-session turns, records outbound Telegram sends into the message-context cache, and adds focused tests plus config-help updates.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection rather than executing tests: current main elides non-bootstrap Codex tool payloads and only records inbound Telegram messages for prompt context, while the PR-introduced raw-probe and reset-toggle regressions are visible in the changed call paths.
commands.review_followup: The contributor needs to add live Telegram proof and the branch needs two prompt-boundary fixes before normal maintainer review; automation should not paper over the missing real-environment proof.

target.title: Preserve Codex context tool evidence
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram prompt context cached inbound messages but not OpenClaw's own outbound Telegram replies, so a later context window could include Keshav's 01:37 request while omitting the adjacent 01:39 assistant answer.
- Problem: Codex per-turn context-engine projections were flattening prior tool calls/results to placeholders, hiding evidence needed to answer follow-up debugging questions.
- Hardening: ordinary fresh Codex/app-server turns now get runtime-loaded daily memory too; this was not the direct 10:11 Telegram cause, because Keshav did not use `/new` or `/reset`.
- Solution: cache successful Telegram outbound sends into the Telegram message-context cache, preserve sanitized tool evidence in active context-engine projections, and load startup daily-memory context for normal first turns in a fresh runtime session.
- Scope boundary: this does not make every old transcript globally searchable; it fixes the observed Telegram outbound-context gap, selected-context payload elision, and a related first-turn daily-memory miss.

## Motivation

Keshav pointed out that a later Codex turn did not show the earlier timeout-halving work or the 01:39 final answer evidence. Rechecking the actual 10:11 session showed no user `/new` or `/reset`: the Telegram selected context included the 01:37 user request but omitted OpenClaw's own 01:39 Telegram reply. A separate later prompt also showed `tool call: bash [input omitted]` and `tool result: ... [content omitted]`.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #85219
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram outbound replies now survive into later Telegram prompt-context windows; Codex context-engine per-turn projections no longer reduce prior tool work to empty placeholders; ordinary fresh runtime turns get daily-memory context.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on this host, branch `fix/codex-context-tool-payloads`, head commit `e8f66a5e06ada3f5716a70afead23fd176d8a05b`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran targeted Vitest coverage plus live production helper checks from the repo root.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied local output from the patched checkout:

```text
contains tool call: true
contains input shape: true
contains useful result: true
contains elision marker: false
contains raw shell command: false
contains raw secret: false
startup prelude loaded: true
model prompt has daily memory: true
model prompt has user ask: true
transcript hides daily memory: true
current inbound context hides daily memory: true
Test Files 6 passed (6)
Tests 342 passed (342)
```

- Observed result after fix: The Telegram send regression records a bot reply in a forum topic, then the next selected Telegram conversation context contains that bot reply body. The projected Codex prompt includes useful result text like `already halved timeoutSeconds=7200`, includes tool input shape metadata, does not include `[content omitted]`, and does not expose raw shell command or secret-like token. A normal first-turn prompt also receives daily-memory text like `timeoutSeconds=7200` while transcript/current inbound context stay clean.
- What was not tested: A full live Telegram-to-Codex production turn was not exercised locally.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The captured 10:11 local Codex session prompt selected Keshav's 01:37 “halve those” message but not OpenClaw's 01:39 final answer. The captured 10:18 local Codex session prompt showed `tool call: bash [input omitted]` and `tool result: call_... [content omitted]`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Telegram only recorded inbound messages in `message-cache.ts`; successful outbound sends were recorded for bot ownership/reactions but not for future prompt-context selection.
- Root cause: `run-attempt.ts` passed `toolPayloadMode: contextEngineProjection ? "preserve" : "elide"`, so active per-turn context-engine projections used elision even when the projection engine had safe redaction support.
- Root cause: startup daily-memory preload was wired for bare `/new` and `/reset`, but not for an ordinary first user message in a fresh runtime session.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No regression covered Telegram outbound replies being present in later selected conversation context, active per-turn context-engine projections preserving redacted tool-result evidence, or daily-memory preload on ordinary first turns.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`, `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: successful Telegram sends are recorded into the persisted prompt-context cache; active context-engine projection preserves useful tool-result evidence while redacting raw command/secret values; ordinary first-turn prompts include startup daily memory while transcripts/current inbound context do not.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it covers the Telegram cache path that lost the 01:39 answer, the Codex prompt sent to `turn/start`, and the gateway/reply prompt assembly paths where related context can be lost.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `context-engine-projection.test.ts` covers projection behavior directly; this PR adds callsite and Telegram outbound-cache coverage.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Future Telegram turns should be able to see OpenClaw's own recent Telegram replies in selected chat context, so a follow-up should not see only the user's earlier request without the bot answer. Codex should also have enough redacted prior tool-result context and fresh-session daily-memory context to avoid repeating completed work when that evidence exists in selected context or today/yesterday memory.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
